title: COURT TRANSCRIPT REQUEST TEMPLATES
subtitle: Templates for Requesting, Managing, and Using Transcript Evidence

template.to: Court Reporter, 14th Circuit Court, 990 Terrace Street, Muskegon, MI 49442
template.subject: REQUEST FOR COURT TRANSCRIPTS — Case No. [CASE_NUMBER]
body_elements: Identification: Andrew James Pigors, Plaintiff, pro se
Purpose: Appeal preparation / Federal complaint evidence
IFP status: If IFP granted, request fee waiver per MCR 8.108(3)
Format: Certified copy, paper and electronic (PDF)
Delivery: Mail to 1977 Whitehall Road, Lot 17, North Muskegon, MI 49445
template.fee_note: Standard rate: ~$3.25/page. IFP waiver applies if granted. Request cost estimate first.

hearings.reason: Foundation of fraud — Emily's original false testimony
McNeill's bias visible in rulings and comments

hearings.hearing: Any hearing where Andrew was denied opportunity to speak

hearings.reason: Lack of required findings per MCL 722.27a(3)
Pattern of collusion between Barnes and McNeill

sections.title: TRANSCRIPT ANALYSIS CHECKLIST
checklist: Identify every instance where McNeill cut off Andrew's testimony
Identify every instance where McNeill accepted Emily's claims without evidence
Identify every ex parte communication referenced
Identify every denial of Andrew's motions
Identify every time McNeill cited a statute or rule — verify accuracy
Identify every factual statement by Emily that contradicts other evidence
Identify every threat or warning directed at Andrew
Identify timeline of events per testimony (for contradiction analysis)
Mark potential perjury with page/line citations
Cross-reference with evidence_quotes table in litigation_context.db

rules.text: Tr. [Date] at p. [page], ll. [lines] (e.g., Tr. 03/15/2024 at p. 47, ll. 5-12)
All transcript excerpts must be from certified copies
Include sufficient surrounding context to avoid misleading excerpts
Quote verbatim — never paraphrase in sworn filings
Attach relevant transcript pages as numbered exhibits
Transcript testimony is NOT hearsay — it's prior testimony (MRE 801(d)(1))
Use transcript inconsistencies to impeach at future hearings (MRE 613)
